hero.title: A public workshop for French democracy.
hero.body: Demos:Cratos helps citizens read the institutions they live under, draft better ones in public, and bring democratic practice back to the municipality.

purpose.label: What this is for
purpose.title: Less civic theatre. More public memory.

pillars.label: Read power
pillars.title: The Fifth Republic, made inspectable
pillars.text: The current Constitution becomes a civic document: articles, history, examples, weak points, and plain-language notes in one place.

pillars.label: Rewrite institutions
pillars.title: A Sixth Republic as public work
pillars.text: Drafts, diffs, forks, review, and visible reasoning turn constitutional writing into a process citizens can follow and contest.

pillars.label: Work locally
pillars.title: Municipal democracy with receipts
pillars.text: Each city gets a public workspace for debates, petitions, studies, votes, budgets, and official answers that do not disappear.

pillars.label: Protect legitimacy
pillars.title: Rules before participation
pillars.text: Identity, moderation, delegation, sortition, and audit trails must protect citizens from capture instead of asking them to trust the platform.

features.label: The working surface
features.title: The site will feel like a civic documentation system before it feels like a social network.

doctrine.label: First doctrine
doctrine.title: Trust should not be a prerequisite.
principles: Participation should end with an answer.
Voting comes after shared facts and visible disagreement.
Identity should prove rights, not expose opinions.
The software should remember what institutions prefer to forget.

constitutionReader.loadingError: The text could not be loaded.

profile.signedOutTitle: Sign in to open your profile.
profile.signedOutBody: Your profile will hold your note proposals, review status, and public author details once moderation is active.
profile.title: Profile
profile.subtitle: Manage the public details attached to your contributions.
profile.bioPlaceholder: A short note about how you read or work on constitutional texts
profile.emptyRequests: No note request yet. Once notes move to Supabase, your proposed changes will appear here.

constitutionReader.title: Constitution Reader
constitutionReader.description: A public reader for the Constitution of 4 October 1958, with structure, search, article navigation, analysis notes, and practical examples of institutional strengths and weaknesses.
nextSteps: Import the full official constitutional text with article-level anchors.
Attach plain-language explanations and historically grounded examples.
Add source references and amendment history for each article.

constitutionWorkshop.title: Constitution Workshop
constitutionWorkshop.description: A collaborative constitutional drafting space inspired by version control: proposals, diffs, branches, forks, comments, and a visual history of how the text evolves.
nextSteps: Model constitutions as structured documents with article-level versions.
Design proposal, review, merge, fork, and moderation workflows.
Create a visual map of drafts and public iteration history.

municipalDemocracy.title: Municipal Democracy
municipalDemocracy.description: A future operational space for cities and citizens: debates, studies, petitions, votes, participation lists, transparent prioritization, and privacy-preserving citizen access.
nextSteps: Define public viewer mode and verified resident permissions.
Explore zero-knowledge identity flows for municipality participation.
Prototype pages such as /lyon for local debates, votes, petitions, and studies.
